title: vpnrouter-gateway configuration (gateway.toml)
description: Desired state for a sing-box edge. mode=gateway (default): TUN L3 edge + nftables (needs interfaces/routing/policies). mode=proxy: mixed inbound, authoring-only (needs proxy/subscription; gateway sections forbidden). Per-mode requirements are enforced by `check`/`validate`, not by this schema.

mode.description: gateway (default): TUN L3 edge. proxy: mixed inbound, authoring-only.

proxy.description: Required iff mode=proxy. The mixed (HTTP-CONNECT/SOCKS) inbound.

listen.description: Listen address for the mixed inbound

port.description: Listen port for the mixed inbound

lan_cidr.description: LAN subnet in CIDR notation, e.g. 192.168.10.0/24

sources.description: Admin host CIDRs: always routed direct, exempt from the killswitch, excluded from tun routing (SSH survives a dead tunnel)

url.description: Subscription URL (secret: embeds a token, redacted in output). Resolve with `resolve-subscription`.

strategy.description: pinned: one selected outbound. urltest: failover group over the whole pool (proxy-mode only).

active.description: Outbound to select. Required iff strategy=pinned; forbidden iff urltest.

mode.description: full: unmatched sources go through vpn; split: unmatched sources go direct

ipv6.description: block: killswitch drops forwarded IPv6 (sing-box is ipv4_only). direct: v6 egresses in the clear (opt-in leak) instead of losing v6.

mtu.description: sing-tun drops IP fragments; >1500 blackholes PMTUD, <1330 drops game DF-UDP

policies.description: First-match-wins in file order: put specific policies before broad ones

enabled.description: Drop vpn-routed LAN traffic toward WAN so it cannot leak when the tunnel is down
